steps.title: Set up the volume formula for the original cylinder
steps.content: For cylinder A with volume 22 cubic centimeters and radius $r_A$ and height $h_A$, we have $\pi r_A^2 h_A = 22$.

steps.title: Apply the new dimensions and calculate
steps.content:
The new cylinder has radius $2r_A$ (twice the radius) and height $\frac{1}{2}h_A$ (half the height). Substituting into the volume formula:
$$V_{\text{new}} = \pi(2r_A)^2 \left(\frac{1}{2}h_A\right)$$
$$= \pi \cdot 4r_A^2 \cdot \frac{1}{2}h_A$$
$$= 2\pi r_A^2 h_A$$
$$= 2 \cdot 22 = 44$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
